items.headline: City Council Authorizes Construction Contract with QLM, Inc.
items.summary: The Town Manager was given authority to execute a construction contract with QLM, Inc., though the specific project details are not fully described in the agenda. Residents should watch for follow-up announcements about what this construction project involves and where it will take place in Los Gatos.

items.headline: City updates Active Transportation Plan with revised scoring criteria
items.summary: Cupertino is developing an updated Active Transportation Plan that will guide biking and walking infrastructure projects. Phase 2 includes new scoring criteria that will determine which pedestrian and cycling projects get built next.

items.headline: Council Vacancy Ordinance Being Changed
items.summary: The City Council is considering amending how council vacancies are filled. The specific changes weren't detailed in the agenda, but this could affect how residents are represented if a council seat opens up.

items.headline: Major elevator upgrades approved at Ridder Park commercial complex
items.summary: A $1.1 million elevator modernization project at 1290 Ridder Park Dr signals significant investment in San José's commercial infrastructure. This type of large-scale building system upgrade typically indicates major office or mixed-use facility improvements that enhance accessibility and safety.

items.headline: Ongoing lawsuit over Mary Avenue safety reaches litigation phase
items.summary: The Garden Gate Coalition is suing the City of Cupertino over Mary Avenue safety concerns. The case is now in Santa Clara Superior Court, indicating a significant dispute between residents and the city over traffic or pedestrian safety on that street.

items.headline: Commercial/industrial project underway at West San Carlos corridor
items.summary: A $696,570 commercial/industrial permit at 1300 W San Carlos St represents activity in San José's industrial zone. Details on the specific use would determine whether this is a new business, expansion, or facility upgrade worth closer attention.

items.headline: City launches business incentive programs for retail and startups
items.summary: Cupertino is rolling out a Business License Amnesty Program, outreach initiatives to shopping centers, and new economic development incentives. The city is also promoting a Summer of Celebrations campaign to encourage shopping locally.

items.headline: City seeks input on community development grant funding priorities
items.summary: Sunnyvale presented applications for HOME and Community Development Block Grant (CDBG) funding to the Housing and Human Services Commission. These grants typically support affordable housing, infrastructure, and services for low-income residents.
